chunks.text: Jean Walrand is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley with contact information including 257 Cory Hall, phone 510-219-5821, and email wlr@eecs.berkeley.edu. He holds a Ph.D. in EECS from UC Berkeley and has authored several books including 'Communication Networks: A Concise Introduction' and 'Probability in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science'. He is a Fellow of the Belgian American Education Foundation and IEEE, and recipient of multiple awards including the INFORMS Lanchester Prize and IEEE Stephen O. Rice Prize.

His research areas include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), performance evaluation, and game theory. He is associated with the Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences (BLISS). Selected publications from 2020 include 'An Architecture for In-Vehicle Network', 'Reduce Delay Variability with an End-to-end Approach', and 'Convergence of a Distributed Kiefer-Wolfowitz Algorithm'.

Notable publications include works on parametric prediction (2018), communication networks textbooks (2017), delay-tolerant services using SDN (2015), token pricing for internet services (2011), and backpressure mechanisms for switch fabrics (2011). Other research covers distributed CSMA scheduling, network security investments, and game theory applications in network design and resource allocation.

Selected honors and awards include the IEEE Koji Kobayashi Computers and Communications Award (2012), IEEE ComSoc Stephen O. Rice Prize (2000), IEEE Fellow (1993), and INFORMS Frederick W. Lanchester Prize (1989). These recognize his contributions to communication networks, performance evaluation, and interdisciplinary research combining electrical engineering, computer science, and operations research.
